req.title: Delete patient (soft) (audit)
req.statement: A system-admin shall soft-delete a patient resource; deletion shall not remove history. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates: (1) admin can soft-delete a Patient resource, (2) history is preserved post-deletion, (3) an AuditEvent is emitted, and (4) non-admins receive 403. Four tests are designed: EP positive (admin deletes patient, verifying 200/204, _history retention, and AuditEvent), NEG negative (non-admin role gets 403), STC boundary (delete an already-deleted patient to test idempotency and state-machine edge), and BVA boundary (patient with exactly one history version before delete, ensuring _history count is precisely 1 prior and 2 after). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up resources.

tests.name: test_admin_soft_delete_patient_emits_audit_event
preconditions: A Patient resource exists with a known ID
AuditEvent endpoint is queryable by entity reference
actions: POST /Patient to create a test patient, capture patient_id
GET /Patient/{patient_id} to confirm resource is marked deleted
GET /Patient/{patient_id}/_history to verify history is retained
expected: GET /Patient/{patient_id} returns 410 Gone with a meta.tag of 'deleted'
AuditEvent exists with action='D' and outcome='0'
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_admin_soft_delete_patient_emits_audit_event(admin_client, create_patient, base_url):
    patient_id = create_patient(admin_client)

    delete_resp = admin_client.delete(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert delete_resp.status_code in (200, 204), f"Expected 200/204, got {delete_resp.status_code}"

    get_resp = admin_client.get(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 410, f"Expected 410 Gone, got {get_resp.status_code}"

    hist_resp = admin_client.get(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}/_history")
    assert hist_resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = hist_resp.json()
    assert bundle.get("total", 0) >= 1, "History must contain at least one entry"

    audit_resp = admin_client.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"Patient/{patient_id}"}
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    entries = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1, "At least one AuditEvent must exist"
    actions = [e["resource"].get("action") for e in entries]
    assert "D" in actions, "AuditEvent with action='D' not found"
    outcomes = [e["resource"].get("outcome") for e in entries if e["resource"].get("action") == "D"]
    assert "0" in outcomes, "AuditEvent for delete must have outcome='0' (success)"

tests.name: test_non_admin_delete_patient_returns_403
preconditions: A Patient resource exists with a known ID
actions: POST /Patient to create a test patient using admin_client, capture patient_id
expected: No deletion AuditEvent with outcome='0' is created for this attempt
trace: R-HLT-079: error pathway - 403 when not admin
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_non_admin_delete_patient_returns_403(admin_client, practitioner_client, create_patient, base_url):
    patient_id = create_patient(admin_client)

    delete_resp = practitioner_client.delete(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert delete_resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {delete_resp.status_code}"

    get_resp = admin_client.get(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200, "Patient must still exist after unauthorized delete attempt"

    audit_resp = admin_client.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"Patient/{patient_id}", "action": "D", "outcome": "0"}
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    entries = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) == 0, "No successful delete AuditEvent should exist after forbidden attempt"

tests.name: test_delete_already_deleted_patient_is_idempotent
preconditions: A Patient resource exists and has already been soft-deleted once
actions: POST /Patient to create a patient, capture patient_id
DELETE /Patient/{patient_id} again (second deletion - already deleted state)
expected: Second DELETE returns 200, 204, or 410 (idempotent, not a 500)
GET /Patient/{patient_id}/_history still returns all history versions
AuditEvent count for this patient is at least 2 (one per delete attempt)
trace: R-HLT-079: deletion shall not remove history
R-HLT-079: state transition - active -> deleted -> deleted (idempotent)
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_delete_already_deleted_patient_is_idempotent(admin_client, create_patient, base_url):
    patient_id = create_patient(admin_client)

    first_delete = admin_client.delete(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert first_delete.status_code in (200, 204), f"First delete failed: {first_delete.status_code}"

    second_delete = admin_client.delete(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert second_delete.status_code in (200, 204, 410), (
        f"Second delete must not cause server error; got {second_delete.status_code}"
    )

    hist_resp = admin_client.get(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}/_history")
    assert hist_resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = hist_resp.json()
    assert bundle.get("total", 0) >= 1, "History must be retained after repeated deletes"

    audit_resp = admin_client.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"Patient/{patient_id}", "action": "D"}
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    entries = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 2, "Both delete operations must each produce an AuditEvent"

tests.name: test_history_version_count_increments_exactly_one_on_delete
preconditions: A freshly created Patient resource exists with exactly one history version (versionId=1)
actions: POST /Patient to create patient; confirm _history total == 1 before delete
expected: The latest history entry has resource.meta.tag containing 'deleted' or entry has no resource element per FHIR spec
trace: R-HLT-079: deletion shall not remove history
R-HLT-079: BVA on minimum history count boundary (1 version before, 2 after)
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_history_version_count_increments_exactly_one_on_delete(admin_client, create_patient, base_url):
    patient_id = create_patient(admin_client)

    pre_hist = admin_client.get(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}/_history")
    assert pre_hist.status_code == 200
    pre_total = pre_hist.json().get("total", 0)
    assert pre_total == 1, f"Expected exactly 1 history entry before delete, got {pre_total}"

    delete_resp = admin_client.delete(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert delete_resp.status_code in (200, 204), f"Delete failed with {delete_resp.status_code}"

    post_hist = admin_client.get(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}/_history")
    assert post_hist.status_code == 200
    post_total = post_hist.json().get("total", 0)
    assert post_total == 2, f"Expected exactly 2 history entries after delete, got {post_total}"

    entries = post_hist.json().get("entry", [])
    latest = entries[0] if entries else {}
    request_method = latest.get("request", {}).get("method", "")
    assert request_method == "DELETE" or "deleted" in str(latest), (
        "Latest history entry must reflect the delete operation"
    )
